---
title: "Search Loop"
subtitle: "LLM-guided exploration of the forecasting pipeline"
---

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
searchData = FileAttachment("_data/search_trajectories.json").json()
searchComparison = FileAttachment("_data/search_comparison.json").json()
pipelineConfigs = FileAttachment("_data/pipeline_configs.json").json()
Plot = import("https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/@observablehq/plot@0.6/+esm")
```

## How the search works

The outer loop uses **Claude** (Sonnet) to propose pipeline configurations. Each iteration:

```
+------------------------------------------+
|  Claude reads: program.md + history      |
|  -> proposes config as JSON              |
+------------------------------------------+
|  Quick eval (20 origins, ~30s)           |
|  -> if worse: reject immediately         |
+------------------------------------------+
|  Full eval (120 origins, ~3min)          |
|  -> if better: ACCEPT, update best       |
|  -> if worse: REJECT                     |
+------------------------------------------+
|  Log iteration, save state, continue     |
+------------------------------------------+
```

The agent receives country-specific domain knowledge and adapts its proposals based on what worked in previous iterations.

## Country

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
viewof selectedCountry = Inputs.radio(
  ["norway", "canada", "sweden"],
  {label: "Country", value: "norway",
   format: v => ({"norway": "Norway", "canada": "Canada", "sweden": "Sweden"})[v]}
)
```

## Search trajectory

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
//| label: fig-trajectory
//| fig-cap: "Search trajectory for the selected country. Green = accepted, red = rejected, orange = error. Green line shows the best score achieved so far."

{
  const countryData = searchData[selectedCountry] || [];

  if (countryData.length === 0) {
    return html`
      <div class="chart-container" style="text-align:center; padding:3rem;">
        <p style="font-size:1.2rem; color:#666;">No search results for this country.</p>
      </div>
    `;
  }

  const data = countryData.map(d => ({
    iteration: d.iteration,
    score: d.full_score || d.quick_score,
    status: d.status,
    description: d.description,
    runtime: d.runtime_seconds
  })).filter(d => d.score != null);

  // Running best
  let best = Infinity;
  const bestLine = data.filter(d => d.status === "accepted").map(d => {
    best = Math.min(best, d.score);
    return {iteration: d.iteration, score: best};
  });

  return Plot.plot({
    height: 400,
    marginLeft: 60,
    x: {label: "Iteration"},
    y: {label: "Score (avg MASE)", grid: true},
    marks: [
      Plot.dot(data, {
        x: "iteration", y: "score",
        fill: d => d.status === "accepted" ? "#2ecc71" : d.status === "rejected" ? "#e74c3c" : "#f39c12",
        r: 5,
        tip: true,
        title: d => `Iter ${d.iteration}\nScore: ${d.score.toFixed(4)}\nStatus: ${d.status}\n${d.description || ''}`
      }),
      Plot.line(bestLine, {
        x: "iteration", y: "score",
        stroke: "#2ecc71", strokeWidth: 2, curve: "step-after"
      }),
    ]
  });
}
```

**Legend:** [Accepted]{style="color:#2ecc71;font-weight:bold"} | [Rejected]{style="color:#e74c3c;font-weight:bold"} | [Error]{style="color:#f39c12;font-weight:bold"}. Green line shows the best score achieved so far.


## Iteration log

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
{
  const countryData = searchData[selectedCountry] || [];

  if (countryData.length === 0) {
    return html`<p style="color:#999;">No iterations to display.</p>`;
  }

  return Inputs.table(countryData.map(d => ({
    "#": d.iteration,
    Status: d.status,
    "Quick Score": d.quick_score?.toFixed(4) || "---",
    "Full Score": d.full_score?.toFixed(4) || "---",
    "Time (s)": d.runtime_seconds?.toFixed(1) || "---",
    Description: d.description || ""
  })), {
    rows: 20,
    sort: "#",
    reverse: true
  });
}
```


## Search method comparison

How do different search strategies compare across all three countries?

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
{
  const methods = ["Informed LLM", "Blind LLM", "Random", "Greedy", "Manual economist", "Zero-shot baseline"];
  const rows = methods.map(m => {
    const row = {Method: m};
    for (const c of ["norway", "canada", "sweden"]) {
      const d = searchComparison.find(r => r.method === m && r.country === c);
      row[c.charAt(0).toUpperCase() + c.slice(1)] = d?.val_mase?.toFixed(3) || "---";
    }
    return row;
  });
  return Inputs.table(rows, {
    columns: ["Method", "Norway", "Canada", "Sweden"],
    width: {Method: 160}
  });
}
```

**Key findings:**

- All search strategies improve over the zero-shot baseline in every country
- No single search strategy dominates across all countries
- The manual economist benchmark is consistently among the weakest --- automated search outperforms hand-selected configurations
